perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties shall execute and perform this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws or regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties shall endeavor to execute and perform this Agreement in general accordance with applicable laws or regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the firm requirement of compliance with laws to a general effort to act in accordance with laws. This weakens the legal obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: New York General Obligations Law requires that contracts that cannot be performed within one year must be in writing. The 'endeavor to execute... in general accordance' language might be interpreted to imply flexibility regarding the written requirement, weakening enforceability and potentially contradicting the statute.

Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 24-A General Obligations ARTICLE 5 Creation, Definition and Enforcement of Contractual Obligations TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability up TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability next SECTION 5-702 Requirements for use of plain language in consumer transactions This entry was published on 2014-09-22 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2015-10-09 2017-10-27 2018-04-20 2018-07-06 2018-07-13 2018-08-31 2019-01-11 2019-01-18 2019-06-28 2019-07-05 2019-07-19 2019-08-16 2019-10-18 2019-11-22 2019-12-13 2020-02-21 2020-04-10 2020-12-18 2021-01-08 2021-04-02 2021-04-09 2021-06-18 2021-09-24 2021-12-31 2022-02-25 2022-03-04 2022-12-30 2023-01-06 2023-10-27 2023-11-26 2024-12-20 2025-01-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 5-701 Agreements required to be in writing General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 7 § 5-701. Agreements required to be in writing. a. Every agreement, promise or undertaking is void, unless it or some note or memorandum thereof be in writing, and subscribed by the party to be charged therewith, or by his lawful agent, if such agreement, promise or undertaking: 1. By its terms is not to be performed within one year from the making thereof or the performance of which is not to be completed before the end of a lifetime; 2. Is a special promise to answer for the debt, default or miscarriage of another person; 3. Is made in consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry; 5. Is a subsequent or new promise to pay a debt discharged in bankruptcy; 6. Notwithstanding section 2-201 of the uniform commercial code, if the goods be sold at public auction, and the auctioneer at the time of the sale, enters in a sale book, a memorandum specifying the nature and price of the property sold, the terms of the sale, the name of the purchaser, and the name of the person on whose account the sale was made, such memorandum is equivalent in effect to a note of the contract or sale, subscribed by the party to be charged therewith; 9. Is a contract to assign or an assignment, with or without consideration to the promisor, of a life or health or accident insurance policy, or a promise, with or without consideration to the promisor, to name a beneficiary of any such policy. This provision shall not apply to a policy of industrial life or health or accident insurance. 10. Is a contract to pay compensation for services rendered in negotiating a loan, or in negotiating the purchase, sale, exchange, renting or leasing of any real estate or interest therein, or of a business opportunity, business, its good will, inventory, fixtures or an interest therein, including a majority of the voting stock interest in a corporation and including the creating of a partnership interest.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall execute and perform... in compliance' to 'shall endeavor to execute... in general accordance' weakens the legal obligation. The original text mandates compliance, whereas the revised text only requires an effort to comply. This creates ambiguity regarding the standard of adherence to laws. While the provided law snippet focuses on agreements needing to be in writing under NY General Obligations Law, the core issue is the diluted commitment to legal compliance. The Perturbation Definition for Ambiguities specifies that a legal contradiction arises when an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law.

perturbation.original_text: The parties irrevocably and unconditionally submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of any state or Federal court sitting in Manhattan, New York over any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties generally submit to the jurisdiction of state or Federal courts sitting in Manhattan, New York over any suit, action or proceeding arguably arising out of this Agreement, unless otherwise agreed.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "generally" and "arguably", the provision loses its force. The phrase 'unless otherwise agreed' introduces further ambiguity, as the conditions for 'otherwise agreed' are not specified.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 28 U.S. Code § 1332 - Diversity of citizenship; amount in controversy; costs
perturbation.law_explanation: This section establishes the jurisdiction of federal courts in cases involving diversity of citizenship and a sufficient amount in controversy. The original clause provides exclusive jurisdiction in New York courts. The modification suggests that jurisdiction may sometimes be elsewhere due to the added ambiguities, potentially conflicting with cases that would otherwise fall under federal diversity jurisdiction according to 28 U.S.C. § 1332.

Diversity of citizenship; amount in controversy; costs (a) The district courts shall have original jurisdiction of all civil actions where the matter in controversy exceeds the sum or value of $75,000, exclusive of interest and costs, and is between- (1) citizens of different States; (2) citizens of a State and citizens or subjects o
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates 'exclusive jurisdiction' in Manhattan, New York. The modified text introduces ambiguities with 'generally,' 'arguably,' and 'unless otherwise agreed,' which weakens the exclusivity. The Law Explanation mentions that this could conflict with federal diversity jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. § 1332. Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines the jurisdiction of district courts in cases of diversity of citizenship. The change undermines the clarity of jurisdiction, potentially leading to disputes over where a case should be heard, which contradicts the original clear designation.
